subject: LU-9397 ksocklnd: move remaining time handling to 64 bits

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Patch Set 2:

The issue with using jiffies is that some kernels now can be "tickless" so HZ is not necessarily a fixed value.  It would be better to convert over to using nsec everywhere, but I can accept that the current code is broken and this needs to be fixed.

Patch Set 2:

I do hav a newer version that converts over to time64_t/ktime_t. Need to test it more first.

parents.subject: LU-9371 tools: handle component options properly

commit.subject: LU-9397 ksocklnd: move remaining time handling to 64 bits
commit.message:
LU-9397 ksocklnd: move remaining time handling to 64 bits

Examination of the ksocklnd time handle revealed that the
code only requires second level precision. Since this is
the case we can move away from using jiffies to time64_t.
This allows us to be independent of the HZ settings in
addition to making it clear what is time handling, using
time64_t verses unsigned long. In the process we remove
many of the various libcfs time wrappers as well.

Change-Id: I968630ef94febd4bff703fb633e677996939f95b
